summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering a full agenda including adoption of the Adams-Clermont Solid Waste District plan, multiple zoning cases (a zone change/PDP for a 230-unit apartment development by PLK Communities which was tabled for further deliberation, and two text amendments that were approved), purchase of copiers, HVAC replacement, and other administrative items. The board also scheduled a special meeting for August 7th to continue the zoning workshop and deliberate on the PLK Communities zone change case.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (June 10 - July 22, 2025) passed unanimously
Resolution R-017-2025 approving the Adams-Clermont Solid Waste District solid waste management plan passed unanimously
Zoning Case ZC-2025-001 (PLK Communities zone change from M1 to PDM) motion to approve died for lack of a second; tabled for August 7 special meeting
Zoning Case ZC-2025-003 text amendments (prohibited activities, Section 5.03) approved unanimously

Zoning Case ZC-2025-004 text amendments (Table 5-2 residential development standards) approved with modifications: R1 single family dwellings changed to 3 acres, all other principal uses kept at 5 acres; passed unanimously
Resolution R-014-2025 authorizing Bob Rose to seek unclaimed funds from Ohio Department of Commerce passed unanimously
Motion to schedule special meeting/zoning workshop for August 7, 2025 at 6 PM passed unanimously
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Sergeant McMillan for homicide investigation training ($1,475) passed unanimously
Motion to purchase two Sharp MX3051 copiers for $2,686 from Millennial Business Systems passed unanimously
Motion to accept resignation of James Pruitt effective June 10, 2025 passed unanimously
Motion to approve HVAC replacement by Tribble Refrigeration at $19,580 plus permit/inspection costs (discussion began, transcript truncated)
action_items: John (Zoning) to meet with Andy Balsizer regarding land splitting question and moratorium implications
Bob Rose to pursue unclaimed funds ($899.06) from Ohio Department of Commerce
Schedule and notify all parties for August 7, 2025 special meeting for zoning workshop and ZC-2025-001 deliberation
PLK Communities to continue working with ODOT on traffic impact study for Smith Road/SR 28
Sergeant McMillan to attend homicide investigation training in Louisville, KY (Jan 5-16, 2026)
Board members to conduct due diligence on M1 zoning loss concerns before August 7 meeting
key_amounts: $899.06 in unclaimed funds from Ohio Department of Commerce
$1,475 registration cost for Sergeant McMillan homicide investigation training
$2,686 to purchase two Sharp MX3051 copiers outright (vs. $18,480 lease over 5 years)
$19,580 plus permit/inspection for HVAC replacement by Tribble Refrigeration
$50 million estimated investment for PLK Communities apartment development
246 units originally proposed (reduced to 230 units) for PLK Communities development on 28.77 acres
$600-$1,800 Flock camera installation cost; $3,500 annual maintenance cost requested by police
Over 11,000 tons of debris removed from Goshen after 2022 storm
140 residents served at new household hazardous waste drop-off (Jan-June)
notable_topics: Adams-Clermont Solid Waste District 2025 plan presentation and ratification
PLK Communities zone change request from M1 to PDM for 230-unit apartment/townhome development on Smith Road
Concern over losing M1 (light manufacturing) zoned land for future economic development
Text amendment to Section 5.03 regarding prohibited activities (nuisance, junk vehicles, vegetation)
Text amendment to Table 5-2 residential development standards (lot sizes and setbacks for R1-R5 districts)
Station 18 fire station construction progress - two weeks ahead of schedule, brick work starting
Beam signing ceremony for new fire station
Clermont County Fair happening in Owensville
Scheduling of August 7 zoning workshop and deliberation session
Flock safety camera requirement discussion for new developments
attendees: Andy Balsizer (public speaker, 1955 Ethel Lynn Lane)
notes: The PLK Communities zone change (ZC-2025-001) motion died for lack of a second due to Trustee Allen wanting more time to evaluate the loss of M1 industrial zoning; the board scheduled an August 7 special meeting to revisit. The Flock camera condition requested by police was discussed but legal counsel advised it may not be appropriate as a land-use zoning condition and could be negotiated separately. The transcript was truncated during discussion of the HVAC replacement item. Peter Kleekamp is the founder of PLK Communities. The development site was shifted from south to north of Smith Road due to jurisdictional wetlands and regulated streams discovered during due diligence.
title: BOT 7-22-25
